title: Sampling, Inference, Regression, and Causal Limits · Spiral Review
summary: Evaluate a program change from sampled observational and experimental evidence. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an inference and limitations brief after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish observational studies, experiments, sampling frames, randomization, and blocking.
Explain sampling variability through simulated sampling distributions.
Construct and interpret confidence intervals without probability misstatements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.
